prompt:
Our MessageCatalog (catalog.ts) has served us fine while everything was English-only flat strings, but the localization push landed two tickets on me at once.

First, plurals. I need catalog.addPlural(locale, key, forms) where forms maps CLDR categories (zero/one/two/few/many/other, nothing else allowed, 'other' mandatory) to templates. t() on a plural key must pick the form using a per-language rule — catalog.setPluralRule(language, fn) registers one, keyed by the language part so 'pl-PL' uses the 'pl' rule — with the default rule being count===1 -> 'one', else 'other'. The count has to arrive as a number in params ({ count: n }, also available for {count} interpolation); anything else is a TypeError, translators keep passing strings and I want it loud. If the rule picks a category the entry doesn't define, use 'other'. Plural entries ride the same locale fallback chain as plain strings, and has() should see them.

Second, namespace fallbacks. Keys are dotted ('admin.save' lives in namespace 'admin'). catalog.addNamespaceFallback('admin', 'common') means: when 'admin.save' can't be found in ANY locale of the chain, retry as 'common.save'. Fallbacks chain transitively and a cycle must terminate cleanly (unknown key still comes back as the key itself). Important ordering call we made in review: the whole locale chain for the exact key is exhausted BEFORE the first namespace rewrite is tried.

test_plurals_fallback.ts is the acceptance suite for both tickets; test_catalog.ts pins the behavior we already ship — nothing in there may regress, and don't edit either test file. node --test test_catalog.ts test_plurals_fallback.ts must go green.
